name: prompts-v1-deprecated
observations: prompts_v1.0.md content should NOT exist as a separate file - content belongs in individual FDQs
prompts_v1.0.md had inconsistencies: EVIDENCE_PACK conflated answer options with Research Pack, system message had explicit anti-hallucination coaching
Design principle: each FDQ is self-contained with its own TASK and OUTPUT_GUARD window content
Shared system prompt is defined at benchmark level, not duplicated or varied per FDQ
Individual FDQs specify only their step-specific content (TASK, OUTPUT_GUARD, placeholders)

name: task-step-definitions-deleted
observations: DELETED: docs/task-step-definitions/ folder (13 files) on 2026-01-27
These were older draft FDQ specifications that were superseded by main FDQ files in docs/ root
Contained inconsistencies with canonical FDQs: wrong chain positions, TBD items, outdated structures
Example inconsistency: 0113_canary.md showed Standalone/TBD chain position vs 03-canary.md showing Position 3

Files deleted: 0113_canary.md, 0113_c-nonexist1.md, 0113_distinguish.md, 0113_fact-extract.md, 0113_irac-no-rp.md, 0113_irac-with-rp.md, 0113_ka-sc.md, 0113_transitive.md, 0113_unknown-auth.md, 0113_validate-auth.md, 0114_fdq-template.md, 0114_synthetic-traps.md, manifest.md
Going forward: ALL FDQ specifications live in docs/ as [legal-10] [fdq] XX-name.md files only

name: fdq-02-c-nonexist1-v3
observations: File: [legal-10] [fdq] 02-c-nonexist1.md serves as the TEMPLATE for FDQ v2.0 structure
Changed from free-text to multiple choice format to avoid needing judge model for scoring - now deterministic
D is the correct answer for all 3 questions (The holding of this case cannot be determined from the materials provided)
Demonstrates professional task framing: no coaching (don't hallucinate), tests natural behavior under pressure to confabulate
Shows complete message assembly pattern that all other FDQs should follow
Synthetic citation generation uses deterministic algorithm based on EU seed for reproducible nonexistent cases
This is the reference implementation that fdq-04-new, fdq-05-new, and fdq-08-new were modeled after

name: fdq-04-fact-extract-v2
observations: File: [legal-10] [fdq] 04-fact-extract-new.md created 2026-01-27 with v2.0 structure
Upgraded from [legal-10] [fdq] 04-fact-extract.md (v1.0) preserving all existing SQL and Python code
Added sections: Purpose, TASK Window Content with fenced format, OUTPUT_GUARD Window Content, full messages array example, Design Rationale
Tests SCDB code interpretation with 2 sub-questions: reversal (from caseDisposition codes) and prevailing_party (from partyWinning codes)
TASK window includes complete SCDB code definitions (11 disposition codes, 4 party winning codes) so model has everything needed to answer

Design Rationale explains: why codes are provided in prompt (tests reading comprehension not memorization), why mixed dispositions = FALSE (deterministic clarity), why JSON response (structured for exact match scoring)
Example uses Brown v. Board of Education, 347 U.S. 483 (1954) with disposition code 3 and party winning code 1
Status: FULLY DEVELOPED and Implementation-Ready

name: fdq-05-distinguish-v2
observations: File: [legal-10] [fdq] 05-distinguish-new.md created 2026-01-27 with v2.0 structure
Upgraded from [legal-10] [fdq] 05-distinguish.md (v1.0) preserving all existing content
Added sections: Purpose with Setup/Test/Failure mode, TASK Window with treatment vocabulary definitions, OUTPUT_GUARD, full messages array, Design Rationale
Tests citation treatment recognition with 2 sub-questions: treatment (8-value enum) and agree (boolean)
Treatment vocabulary FROZEN in TASK window: FOLLOWS, DISTINGUISHES, QUESTIONS, CRITICIZES, OVERRULES, LIMITS, CITES, EXPLAINS
Selection logic uses highest-authority cited case by Fowler score to test treatment of most important precedent

Design Rationale explains: why highest-Fowler selection (substantive treatment more likely), why 8-value vocabulary (matches Shepard's exactly), why separate treatment and agreement (distinct dimensions)
Example uses Planned Parenthood v. Casey citing Roe v. Wade with treatment=FOLLOWS, agree=TRUE
Status: FULLY DEVELOPED and Implementation-Ready

name: fdq-08-transitive-v2
observations: File: [legal-10] [fdq] 08-transitive-new.md created 2026-01-27 with v2.0 structure
Upgraded from [legal-10] [fdq] 08-transitive.md (v1.0) - CRITICAL: preserved ALL complex SQL (3-way CTE joins), ALL Python code, TRANS-TREATMENT comparison table, complete example
Tests triangle citation prediction: given B->A treatment and C->B treatment, predict C->A treatment
Ground truth is ACTUAL C->A Shepard's edge - NOT theoretical expectation matrix (this distinction is critical and preserved from v1.0)
Design Notes section preserved explaining why this works unlike rejected TRANS-TREATMENT approach
Complex eligibility SQL preserved: finds complete triangles where all 3 edges exist with temporal order A < B < C
Example uses Miranda v. Arizona (A) <- Harris v. New York (B) <- Oregon v. Hass (C) triangle
Added sections: Purpose, TASK Window Content, OUTPUT_GUARD, placeholder mapping table, expanded Design Rationale
Status: FULLY DEVELOPED and Implementation-Ready

name: fdq-v2-structure
observations: FDQ v2.0 is the MANDATORY structure for all Legal-10 benchmark question specifications as of 2026-01-27
Template derived from FDQ-02 v3.0 ([legal-10] [fdq] 02-c-nonexist1.md) which demonstrates the canonical pattern

Required sections in order: Question ID + metadata, Purpose (Setup/Test/Failure mode), System Prompt, TASK Window Content, OUTPUT_GUARD Window Content, Data Requirements, Eligibility Criteria, Instantiation, Response Format + Parsing, Scoring, Example Instance (with FULL messages array), Design Rationale, FDQ Checklist
TASK and OUTPUT_GUARD windows use fenced format: <<<BEGIN_TASK>>>...<<<END_TASK>>> and <<<BEGIN_OUTPUT_GUARD>>>...<<<END_OUTPUT_GUARD>>>
Example Instance section MUST show the complete messages array assembly: messages[0] through messages[N] with role and content for each
Purpose section documents three components: Setup (what model has), Test (what we're testing), Failure mode (how model can fail)
Design Rationale section explains WHY design decisions were made - not just WHAT but WHY
FDQ Checklist is a completion gate ensuring all required elements are present before marking Implementation-Ready
All existing content from v1.0 FDQs MUST be preserved when upgrading - SQL, Python code, examples are NEVER summarized or weakened
System Prompt references shared benchmark-level system prompt - FDQs do not define their own system prompts

name: message-assembly-windows
observations: Message assembly uses fenced windows with BEGIN/END markers as defined in [C] [platform] agchain system architecture.md
Canonical window order: ENV -> ANCHOR_PACK -> EVIDENCE_PACK -> CARRY_FORWARD -> TASK -> OUTPUT_GUARD
Each window is a SEPARATE message in the messages array with role='user' (except system prompt which is role='system')
messages[0] is ALWAYS the system prompt (role='system') - invariant across all steps
messages[1] is ENV window with benchmark metadata (benchmark name, step number, step_id)
ANCHOR_PACK contains p1 payload content - the anchor opinion text for legal-10
EVIDENCE_PACK contains p2 payload content when admitted - Research Pack authorities for open-book steps
CARRY_FORWARD contains candidate_state from prior steps for chained reasoning
TASK window contains the FDQ-specific question/task content with placeholders like {CITED_CASE_NAME}
OUTPUT_GUARD window specifies exact JSON response format and parsing contract
FDQ documents must show messages array in Example Instance section demonstrating this assembly
